artifacts.summary: Maintenance recheck of the UWA student AI guide PDF (src-uwa-002 from run 20260513). Content hash changed from aecf591a0b5e39661c96b374c2dbcf4ddd6fcc67601adf912458167884fb893c (May 13) to 7d754da317549a66ae03290631c1d199876d5b89fe3c00cf16431b6de471a46c (July 7). Source remains accessible and publicly downloadable via direct link without auth. The DeepSeek ban has been added to this consolidated PDF, along with expanded privacy/IP warnings. Core Tier 1/Tier 2/Tier 3 guidance and acknowledgement requirements remain substantively consistent with the May 2026 snapshot.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Using Artificial Intelligence Tools at UWA: A Guide for Students
Using Artificial Intelligence Tools at UWA: A Guide for Students
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Additionally, not all AI platforms are safe to use. The Department of Home Affairs has raised concerns on the safety and security of data shared with the AI tool DeepSeek. As such, UWA staff and students must not use DeepSeek in any way. UWA has blocked the use of DeepSeek on all University-managed networks and devices, and students should avoid using DeepSeek on their personal devices.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: It is also important that you do not input any intellectual property or copyright material that is not your own, including the intellectual property of UWA or its staff – such as lecture slides, handouts, and unit reading material – as it is not yours to distribute.

artifacts.claimText: UWA’s 2026 student AI guide now states that UWA staff and students must not use DeepSeek in any way, that UWA has blocked DeepSeek on University-managed networks and devices, and that students should avoid using DeepSeek on their personal devices.

artifacts.title: Maintenance review: UWA 2026 student AI guide PDF
artifacts.summary: Local repair review validated two policy-content updates in UWA's 2026 student AI guide PDF: the consolidated DeepSeek prohibition and expanded privacy/IP guidance that tells students not to input UWA staff teaching materials into public AI systems.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
This repair used the public current PDF and the July 7 maintenance candidate bundle; it does not publish raw PDF text.
The DeepSeek prohibition overlaps with an existing askUWA-sourced claim and is released here as additional source-specific evidence from the consolidated student guide.
